'''Crispin Hoedaack''' was a functionary and the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the [[New Galactic Empire]]'s attempt to annex the [[Aquilaean system]]. He was entitled [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] and [[First Lord of the Aquilaean System and Surrounding Territories]].

==Biography==
Crispin Hoedaack had initially served as a [[Governor/Legends|governor]] within the [[New Galactic Empire]]. However, nearing the start of the Aquilae Campaign, Hoedaack was promoted to the rank of [[First Lord of the Aquilaean System and Surrounding Territories]] by the reigning [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Cos Dashit]], an arrangement that Dashit and Hoedaack had agreed to in private earlier. Before the speech, Hoedaack informed Dashit that the crowds had been awaiting at the [[Plaza of the Daders]] all morning. Secretly, however, he alongside [[Darth Vader (The Star Wars)|Darth Vader]] and [[Vantos Coll]] plotted to usurp Dashit's authority. Eventually, Hoedaack perished when his [[Death Star/Legends|space fortress]] was destroyed by [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] and [[Luke Skywalker (The Star Wars)|Luke Skywalker]] in orbit around [[Aquilae (The Star Wars)|Aquilae]].

==Personality and traits==
He was a young man with angular features and gray eyes, as well as blond hair and fair skin. Due to his status as a member of the cabal that replaced the [[Great Families (The Star Wars)|Great Families]], which formerly made up the [[Supreme Tribunal]], he was also extremely ambitious. Hoedaack hoped to gain the directorship with the assistance of his allies, Darth Vader and Vantos Coll.

He wore a black and red military uniform with gold shoulder pauldrons at the time of his promotion.

==Behind the scenes==
Hoedaack's surname was slightly modified and assigned to [[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross Holdaack]] in the first draft. He is the precursor of [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]]. The name Tarkin was assigned to a [[Tarkin (The Star Wars)|religious figure]] on [[Aquilae (The Star Wars)|Aquilae]] in the rough draft.
